page.title: Measles — RepurpOS | OpenSourceMedicine
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for Measles: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for Measles: 24 alterations and 11 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: "The design and reporting of safety outcomes in MMR vaccine studies, both pre- and post-marketing, are largely inadequate. "
Mortality rates in girls were higher after receiving the inactivated measles vaccine.
Thimerosol-containing vaccines are associated with autism prevalence and measles-containing vaccines are associated with serious neurological disorders.
Vaccination is associated with a rare autoimmune neurological condition transverse myelitis.
MMR vaccination rates have been found higher in CD patients (90.3%), compared to UC patients and the controls (74.8 and 67.3%, respectively)
"A history of prior vaccination is not always associated with immunity nor with the presence of specific antibodies."
"Infectious vaccine-derived rubella viruses emerge, persist, and evolve in cutaneous granulomas of children with primary immunodeficiencies."
"Polymorphisms in the Wilms Tumor Gene Are Associated With Interindividual Variations in Rubella Virus–Specific Cellular Immunity After Measles-Mumps-Rubella II Vaccination"
20.7% (6 of 29) of persons known to have received measles vaccine had non-protective titers.
92% of mumps cases in a US mumps outbreak were children who were previously vaccinated, raising questions about the effectiveness and waning immunity of the mumps vaccine.
A 1993 outbreak of measles in a highly immunised Australian population.
A group of vaccine-induced immune prepubertal children will not always prevent the spread of rubella into the rest of the community.
A low measles vaccine efficacy rate may explain the less-than-expected gains attributable to vaccination.
A major measles epidemic occured in 1989 in the region of Quebec despite a 99% vaccine coverage.
A measles outbreak at a college with a prematriculation immunization requirement.
A measles outbreak in Montana in 1985 indicates vaccine failure.
A measles outbreak was reported in a highly vaccinated population, San Diego, 2008
Administration of varicella vaccine before the age of 15 months, and the prescription of oral steroids, may be associated with a slightly increased risk of breakthrough disease.
All children diagnosed with behavioral and developmental regression presented with intestinal abnormalities some of which were associated with the MMR vaccine, according to this study.

Among 12- to 23-month-olds who received their first dose of measles-containing vaccine, fever and seizure were elevated 7 to 10 days after vaccination. Vaccination with MMRV results in 1 additional febrile seizure for every 2300 doses.
An outbreak of measles occurred in a high school with a documented vaccination level of 98 per cent.
As measles immunization rates rise to high levels in a population, measles becomes a disease of immunized persons.
Autistic children have elevated levels of measles antibodies indicating that measles vaccination may be causing autoimmunity in these children.
Cases of vaccine-strain positive measles in Toronto, Canada, 2015.
Certain single-nucleotide polymorphisms are associated with immune responses to measles and rubella vaccines.
Children under 2 have significantly lower seroprevalence rates after measles vaccination compared to other age groups.

Children with measles studied in this New York City outbreak had lower vitamin A levels and were more likely to have a high fever for more than 7 days, be hospitalized, have lower measles antibodies, and increased morbidity.
Combined MMR and varicella live vaccine is associated with higher rates of febrile convulsion than giving the vaccines separately.
Despite a 95% coverage rate of two measles-containing vaccine doses, endemic measles persists in China.
Despite a 95% coverage rate, Brazil had a measles epidemic in 2013.
Despite a high coverage with measles vaccines in parts of west Africa, epidemics of measles occur with reduced severity in an increasing proportion of older children who have been vaccinated.

Despite high population immunity among children and adolescents following three rounds of measles vaccine supplementary immunization activities, sustained measles virus transmission still occurred among adults in this community.
Detection of measles vaccine in the throat of a vaccinated child.
Detection of modified measles and super-spreader using a real-time reverse transcription PCR in the largest measles outbreak, Yamagata, Japan.
Difficulties in eliminating measles and controlling rubella and mumps in a 99% measles vaccine compliant population.
Diphtheria-tetanus-pertussis vaccine administered simultaneously with measles vaccine is associated with increased morbidity and poor growth in girls.
During vaccine campaigns, children who are already immune to measles often receive an additional and unnecessary vaccine.
Early loss of passive measles antibody in infants of mothers with vaccine-induced immunity.
Early measles vaccination results in reduced long-term neutralizing antibody responses that isn't improved by subsequent vaccination.
European and Hispanic individuals have a much lower antibody response to rubella vaccination.
Even though 95% of the children had measles antibodies after vaccination, vaccine efficacy was not more than 68%.
Fifty-one percent of cases of patients in a 1998/1999 mumps outbreak had at least one MMR vaccination, indicating their effectiveness may be overestimated.
High titre measles vaccination increases female mortality in those receiving immunization in West Africa.
High-titer measles vaccination before 9 months of age has been linked to increased female mortality.
In a measles outbreak from March 1991 to April 1992 in Rio de Janeiro 76.4% of those suspected to be infected had received measles vaccine before their first birthday.
Incidence of adverse reactions to vaccines in pediatric populations are under-reported and may be as high as 43.4% for certain vaccine combinations.
Laboratory characterization of measles virus infection in previously vaccinated and unvaccinated individuals.
Measles and mumps, especially in case of both infections, were associated with lower risks of mortality from atherosclerotic CVD.
Measles antibodies are higher in the cord blood of unvaccinated, older mothers who were likely infected with the wild measles virus, producing better immunity.
Measles can occur in individuals who have received two doses of the measles vaccine and with adequate pre-exposure antibodies.
Measles infection protects against allergic disease in children.
Measles infections still affect vaccinated individuals.
Measles outbreak in a fully immunized secondary-school population with up to 99 percent vaccination.
Measles Outbreak in a Highly Vaccinated Population - Israel, July-August 2017.
Measles titers decline at an average rate of 9.7% per year.

Measles virus DNA from the MMR vaccine has been found in peripheral mononuclear cells in patients with ulcerative colitis and children with autism, indicating its possible role in the pathogenesis of these disorders.
Measles, mumps, and rubella catch up immunisation in a measles epidemic did not appear to confer protection and was associated with a variety of new side effects of the vaccine.
Measles-mumps-rubella vaccine that is given in the second year of life is associated with an increased risk of immune thrombocytopenia purpura.
MMR vaccination is associated with an increased risk for idiopathic thrombocytopaenic purpura.
MMR vaccination is associated with an increased risk of developing acute immune thrombocytopenia in childhood.
Mumps orchitis occurs vaccinated postpubertal males.
Mumps parotitis may lead to expression and immune recognition of a tumor-associated form of MUC1 and create effective immune surveillance of ovarian cancer cells that express this form of MUC1.
Nucleic acid testing identified that a vaccine-type measles virus was being shed in the urine.
Only 33.8% of those born after 1981-1990 have vaccine-induced IgG levels considered protective of rubella.
Outbreaks of mumps between 2009 and 2011 were attributed to secondary vaccine failure.
Passive acquired immunity against measles in infants born to naturally infected and vaccinated mothers.
Passive immunity against measles is superior in infants of mothers who experienced naturally acquired measles infection versus those who were vaccinated.
Primary and secondary vaccine failure may explain the 1992 measles epidemic in Cape Town.
Primary vaccine failures of the measles vaccine is stalling its "eradication".
Reduced antibody responses to the measles vaccine as a result of early administration persist after the second dose.
Ribavirin and high dose vitamin A might be a treatment option for measles pneumonitis in adults.
Routine vaccination has skewed measles incidence to the 18-24 year population, a population of which a higher rate of complications is expected.
Secondary measles-vaccine failures are more common than was more previously thought, particularly among individuals vaccinated in early life, long ago, and among re-vaccinees.
Sensitization to bovine serum albumin is possibly playing a role in allergic reactions to vaccines.
Several investigators have shown that the presence of anti-rubella antibody is not sufficient to eliminate rubella virus.
Simultaneous administration of MMR and DTaP-IPV-Hib compared with MMR alone may increase the rate of hospital admissions related to lower respiratory tract infections.
Some rubella vaccinees never reach or maintain protective antibody levels. This may be explained by polymorphisms in the HLA-DPB1 gene.
The combination of MMR and DTaP-Hib-IPV vaccination is associated with significantly increased rates of adverse effects.
The effects of air pollution and meteorological factors on measles cases in Lanzhou, China.
The Institute of Medicine reported a causal relationship between the rubella vaccine and acute and chronic arthritis.
The largest measles outbreak, including 38 modified measles and 22 typical measles cases in its elimination era in Yamagata, Japan, 2017.
The measles vaccine can induce encephalitis.
The measles-mumps-rubella vaccine causes autoantibodies to be formed against myelin basic protein (the protective coating of the nerves) contributing to the pathogenesis of austim.
The number of measles cases has begun to increase since 2011 after a decline.
The occurrence of secondary vaccine failure and vaccine-modified measles in the United States may lead to underreporting of measles cases and result in overestimation of vaccine efficacy in h

The risk of febrile seizures doubles with the MMRV vaccine compared to the MMR and varicella administered separately. Despite this risk, this paper still emphasizes the fact that MMRV enhances vaccine uptake due to preference of combination vaccines.
The wakefield scare created a natural experiment that may demonstrate a causal relationship between fetal cell line manufactured vaccines and ASD prevalence.
There is an elevated risk of Henoch-Schönlein purpura associated with MMR vaccination.
There is an increased risk of fever following the measles vaccine.
There is evidence that measles vaccine recipients can shed measles virus.
There is little information on the safety of the second dose of mumps vaccine.
This analysis of plasma donors indicates that the measles vaccine has become increasingly ineffective.

This study analyzed restricted access CDC data and found that African American boys vaccinated with the MMR vaccine before the age of 24 months and after the age of 36 months were more likely to receive an autism diagnosis.
This study concludes that the measles vaccine plays a part in the development of Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis.
This study confirms a causal association between idiopathic thrombocytopenia purpura and the MMR vaccine.
This study confirms previous findings suggesting a higher mortality rate when DTP-containing vaccines are received with or after the live measles vaccine.
This study identified 130% more cases of measles among 2-dose measles vaccination recipients.
This study identified genes that may predict measles vaccine failure.
This study provides new evidence that measles incidence is associated with exposure to ambient fine particles in China.
Thrombocytopenic purpura following vaccination in early childhood has been reported.
Two reports of children with measles after vaccination, testing positive for genotype A.
Undernutrition as an underlying cause of child deaths associated with diarrhea, pneumonia, malaria, and measles.
Vaccination may not account entirely for transmission reductions, suggesting that changes in community behavior (social distancing) and other control efforts (isolation, quarantining) are important.
Vaccination timing and co-administration may be associated with increased mortality, especially in females.
Vaccination with the MMR vaccine resulted in acute encephalopathy in 48 child, resulting in either death or severe neurological sequelae.
Vaccination-associated adverse events occur in approximately 1 of every 6 toddlers receiving measles-mumps-rubella vaccine dose 1, with high fever occurring in 1 of 20
Vaccine-associated measles has been identified in Zhejiang.
Vitamin A supplementation may have therapeutic activity in the treatment of measles and respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) infection.
Waning levels of measles antibodies with increasing time post-vaccination suggests that measles susceptibility is potentially increasing in Korea.
Infection of human B lymphocytes with MMR vaccine induces IgE class switching.
"Mumps outbreak among vaccinated university students associated with a large party, the Netherlands, 2010."
A case report of a 10-month-old experiencing purpura and acute gastrointestinal bleeding following MMR vaccination.
A case report of a unilateral anterior uveitis and cataract 3 months after an MMR vaccine.
A case report of acute disseminated myelitis following live rubella vaccination.
A case report of bilateral deafness as a complication of vaccination.
A case report of bilateral optic neuritis 18 days after administration of the MMR vaccine.
A case report of complex regional pain syndrome type-I after rubella vaccination.
A case report of fatal encephalitis asssociated with the measles rubella vaccine.
A case report of pancreatitis after an MMR II vaccine.
A case report of parkinsonism following measles vaccination.
A case report of rapid onset optic neuritis within a few hours of measles-rubella vaccination.
A case report of recurrent benign 6th nerve palsy following measles-mumps-rubella and varicella vaccines, given on separate occasions.
A case report of systemic juvenile idiopathic arthritis relapse after rubella vaccination.

A case repot of a female infant with undiagnosed severe combined immunodeficiency who presented with disseminated vaccine-acquired varicella (VZV) and vaccine-acquired rubella infections at 13 months of age.
A patient that had received 2 doses of MMR vaccine developed measles after being exposed in the setting of a hospital waiting room.
A post vaccine case report involving an adverse event and the presence of a microdeletion of chromosome 22 linked to the DiGeorge syndrome.
Acute optic neuritis is a rare complication of MR vaccination and may occur early after immunization.
Anaphylactic reactions to measles-mumps-rubella vaccine in three children with allergies to hen's egg and cow's milk.
Case report: an outbreak of measles among persons with prior evidence of immunity, New York City, 2011.
Deep sequencing reveals persistence of cell-associated mumps vaccine virus in chronic encephalitis.
Measles vaccine and glyphosate-induced parkinsonism has been reported.
The case reported describes a partial recurrent oculomotor palsy associated with systemic symptoms following MMR vaccination in a healthy young child.
This case report details vaccine-induced measles.

This case study describes a case of measles that was genotyped as being caused by the vaccine strain. The symptoms developed 37 day post-vaccination which exceeds the normal incubation period of 8-12 days for wild-type measles.

This case study describes a recently vaccinated child who developed a vaccine-strain measles infection. The author indicates the need for virus detection as opposed to antibody screening to differentiate between infection caused by vaccines and wild-type.
This paper described cases of nonfebrile seizures associated with the MMR vaccines. Vaccine-strain viruses were identified.
This patient developed optic neuritis about five days after being given a dose of MMR vaccine.
Antibodies formed to rubella virus induce demyelination in rat brain cells, indicating that infection with and/or vaccination against rubella may induce autoimmune demyelination.
In this animal study measles vaccine did not prevent infection or disease against wild type MeV.
In vitro and in vivo growth alter the population dynamic and properties of a Jeryl Lynn mumps vaccine.
Interferon gamma protects the neonatal brain from measles-induced damage. The adaptive immune response is detrimental in the absence of interferon gamma.
Ginkgolic acid inhibits fusion of enveloped viruses.
A defined anthocyanin mixture sourced from bilberry and black currant inhibits Measles virus and various herpesviruses.
A personal or family (such as a sibling or parent) history of seizures is now a precaution for MMRV vaccination.
A review of vaccine contamination in 4 vaccines.
Aborted fetal cells (diploid) have been used to create the rubella, measles, mumps, rabies, polio, smallpox, hepatitis A, chickenpox, and herpes zoster vaccines.
Are cases of mumps in vaccinated patients attributable to mismatches in both vaccine T-cell and B-cell epitopes?: An immunoinformatic analysis.
Autoimmune autistic disorder, a major subset of autism, is associated with autoantibody formation caused by viral (wild and vaccine-induced) infection.
Biological assays lend support to the association between measles virus or MMR and autism.
Calcium spirulan, an inhibitor of enveloped virus replication, from a blue-green alga Spirulina, exhibits activity against a variety of viruses.

disclaimer: Alterations and therapeutics are aggregated from public databases (Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, DGIdb, and others). Natural agents are candidates from OSMF narrative reviews on opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease.html — not approved treatments. This page is for research orientation only — not medical advice.
